commit.message:
feat: server-side OAuth token exchange proxy via Vercel

Intervals.icu requires client_secret on the server side. Instead of
embedding it in the browser bundle, add a Vercel serverless function at
/api/oauth/token that holds the secret as a server env var (OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET).

Changes:
- api/oauth/token.ts: new POST handler that proxies token exchange to
  Intervals.icu, with CORS support via OAUTH_ALLOWED_ORIGIN env var
- vercel.json: route /api/oauth/token before the catch-all /api/(.*) rule
- src/lib/oauth.ts: use VITE_OAUTH_PROXY_URL when set, fall back to
  VITE_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET for static-only deployments
- .github/workflows/deploy.yml: inject VITE_OAUTH_PROXY_URL from secrets
- .env.example: document both configuration options with setup steps

Setup required:
  Vercel env vars: OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET, OAUTH_ALLOWED_ORIGIN
  GitHub secret:   VITE_OAUTH_PROXY_URL=https://<vercel-url>/api/oauth/token

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: extract load_pretrain_weights + apply_lora into models/weights.py (#840)

Eliminates duplicated weight-loading and LoRA application logic between
detr.py (_load_pretrain_weights_into, _apply_lora_to) and module_model.py
(_load_pretrain_weights, _apply_lora).  The canonical unified implementation
lives in the new src/rfdetr/models/weights.py and both callers delegate to it.

Key changes:
- New src/rfdetr/models/weights.py: load_pretrain_weights() uses the more
  complete module_model.py logic (Pydantic model_fields_set for user-override
  detection, auto-align to fine-tuned checkpoints, expand-back for explicit
  num_classes) plus class_names extraction from detr.py.  apply_lora() is
  the unified LoRA application function.
- detr.py: removes ~80 lines of private weight/LoRA methods; delegates to
  weights.py; fixes mutable COCO_CLASS_NAMES returned directly from
  class_names property (now returns list copy).
- module_model.py: removes ~95 lines of private weight/LoRA methods;
  delegates to weights.py.
- tests/models/test_weights.py: 8 new characterization tests (4 reinit
  scenarios, 2 class_names extraction, 2 LoRA application).
- test_load_pretrain_weights.py, test_module_model.py, test_detr_shim.py:
  migrated patch targets and call sites to rfdetr.models.weights.
- Pin torchvision>=0.17.0 (aligns with torch>=2.2.0 floor)
- Pin pydantic>=2.0,<3 (formalises existing v2-only API usage)
- Move peft from core deps to [lora] and [train] optional extras
- Replace top-level `from peft import PeftModel` in backbone.py with
  lazy try/except import inside Backbone.export()
- Fix merge_and_unload() result not being assigned back to self.encoder
- Add ImportError hints in _apply_lora_to() and _apply_lora() pointing
  users to `pip install rfdetr[lora]`
- Add characterization test: importing backbone must not pull peft into
  sys.modules
- Add regression test: Backbone.export() replaces encoder with merged model
- `_RemovedModuleFinder.find_spec` now short-circuits on any `fullname`
  that does not start with `rfdetr.`, preventing bare top-level imports
  such as `import util` or `import deploy` from being hijacked by the
  RF-DETR migration finder after `import rfdetr`.
- Replace the type-identity dedup guard with a `sys`-level sentinel
  (`sys._rfdetr_removed_finder`) so `importlib.reload(rfdetr)` never
  inserts a second finder into `sys.meta_path`.
- Add two characterization tests: scope guard (bare names return None)
  and reload idempotency (finder count unchanged after reload).

---------

commit.message:
fix: replace fragile background task auto-resume with SDK-driven idle detection

Remove ~500 lines of heuristic background task tracking that caused
infinite loop crashes. The old system tracked every async powershell
and background agent launch, then auto-triggered follow-up turns via
SendAsync — this fired for normal tool usage and re-armed endlessly.

The new approach uses SessionIdleEvent.Data.BackgroundTasks from the
Copilot SDK, which reports pending shells/agents directly. When
background work is pending, HasPendingBackgroundWork keeps the session
alive across chat switches. When all work completes, the SDK auto-triggers
follow-up turns and Lumi shows unread indicators and OS notifications.

No custom tools, no counters, no tracking — just one SDK data check.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(debt-service): align interest recalculation with batch accrual logic

Three correctness gaps closed in InterestRecalculationServiceImpl:

1. Per-debt rate key resolution
   Previously hardcoded 'RATE_INDR_STD' for all debts. Now resolves the
   correct config key (RATE_INDR_STD, RATE_INDR_TOLD, RATE_INDR_TOLD_AFD,
   etc.) from the debt's interest_rule field using the same logic as
   InterestAccrualJobHelper.resolveRate(). Exempt debts (INDR_EXEMPT) and
   contractual-rate debts correctly return zero / skip recalculation.

2. Fee-inclusive interest base
   The daily batch adds feesAmount to outstandingBalance before calculating
   interest (gældsinddrivelsesloven §13). The recalculation path was using
   outstandingBalance only, producing lower recalculated interest than the
   original entries whenever fees existed. Now mirrors the batch exactly.

3. Monthly rate boundary re-check
   Rate was re-resolved only on the first day of the window and on 1 Jan.
   This missed mid-year NB-rate changes (e.g. a Nationalbanken announcement
   on 2025-07-07) in multi-month recalculation windows. Rate is now
   re-queried at the start of every calendar month (getDayOfMonth() == 1),
   guaranteeing correct rate boundaries for windows of any length.

Also adds accountingTarget=FORDRINGSHAVER to recalculated entries,
consistent with the batch job.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(security): add @Profile('dev') guard to portal SecurityConfig beans

Both portal SecurityConfig classes used anyRequest().permitAll() with
no profile guard in code. Security was toggled only via YAML
autoconfiguration exclusions in application-dev.yml, meaning a
misconfigured deployment passing --spring.profiles.active=dev would
silently open all endpoints.

Adding @Profile('dev') to the SecurityConfig class ensures the open
SecurityFilterChain is ONLY registered when the dev profile is
explicitly active. Without it, Spring Security's default deny-all
behaviour applies, forcing proper Keycloak OAuth2 configuration.

This matches the profile-aware pattern already used in backend services
(debt-service, payment-service, etc.).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(caseworker-portal): wrap posteringslog table in overflow-x:auto container

The 8-column posteringslog table overflowed the card's right edge on
typical viewport widths because the sidebar layout (16rem nav + gap)
reduces the available content width. Wrapping the table in a scrollable
div keeps the card width intact and lets the table scroll horizontally
inside the card boundary.

commit.message:
fix(psql): set PGCLIENTENCODING=UTF8 to prevent Danish character corruption

Without explicit UTF-8 client encoding, Windows psql defaults to WIN1252,
causing Danish characters (æøå) to be stored as Mojibake in the database
(e.g. ae becomes the 2-byte sequence C383C2A6 instead of C3A6).

This caused seed migration V6 to insert garbled text when applied manually
via psql before Flyway ran on service startup. Fixed the resulting duplicate
rows (garbled + clean) in opendebt_case by deleting garbled rows and
deduplicating exact-content doubles via ctid.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add download-index command for pre-built index from GitHub Releases

Users can now get semantic search in ~30 seconds instead of 15-30 minutes:
  npx @jasonbellz/gospel-library-mcp download-index

Downloads index.db from the latest GitHub Release and saves it to
~/.gospel-library-mcp/index.db. Uses atomic write (.tmp + rename)
to avoid corruption if interrupted.

Also:
- Export DB_DIR from vectorStore.ts (needed by downloader)
- Update startup/search fallback messages to suggest download-index first
- Update build-index message to reflect title+description indexing

commit.message:
fix(caseworker-portal): replace invalid #maps.values() with map entry iteration

Thymeleaf's #maps utility does not expose a values() method. The template
was calling #maps.values(grouped) in two places (Renter and Gebyrer table
sections), causing a SpEL EL1004E exception and a 500 on /konfiguration.

Fixed by iterating the map entries directly — the same pattern already used
correctly in the third table section:

  th:each="mapEntry : \" → th:each="e : \"

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf(debt-service): enable JDBC batch inserts for interest accrual job

Without JDBC batching, saveAll(1000 entries) fires 1000 individual INSERT
round-trips per page. With batch_size=100 + order_inserts=true Hibernate
groups them into 10 batched statements instead, reducing DB round-trips by
10x per page and expected total job runtime from ~20 min to ~4-6 min for
1M debts.

  hibernate.jdbc.batch_size=100   — batch size for INSERT/UPDATE statements
  hibernate.order_inserts=true    — required for batching to activate
  hibernate.order_updates=true    — prevents unnecessary flush/batch breaks

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(payment-service): make V5 ledger migration idempotent

data seeding, causing Flyway's V5 migration to fail on service startup with
'column already exists'. Changed ADD COLUMN to ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS so
V5 can run safely whether or not the column was already created.

current intake and daily protein goal. This adds a dynamic tips section
that surfaces food suggestions calibrated to the remaining amount.

feat: index title + og:description for richer semantic embeddings

Previously only the article title was embedded. Now each document is
indexed as 'Title. Description' using the og:description meta tag,
which contains a meaningful summary of the article content.

This significantly improves semantic search recall — queries about
concepts mentioned only in the article body (not the title) now match
correctly. Existing indexes should be rebuilt with build-index.

Also includes entity decoding for meta tag content (& etc.) and
expanded Range header from 8KB to 16KB to capture longer <head> sections.

fix: background task keepalive tracks shell/agent completion, not just launch

The background auto-resume feature was broken when switching chats because
PendingBackgroundTaskCount was decremented too early (on launch completion
rather than on final read_powershell/read_agent completion). This caused
ReleaseInactiveChatState to dispose the session subscription before the
SDK could deliver follow-up events.

Changes:
- Track shell IDs from async PowerShell launches and agent IDs from
  background task launches separately
- Only decrement PendingBackgroundTaskCount when the final read result
  is observed (exit code marker for shells, status: completed/failed
  for agents)
- Mark assistantMessageAddedSinceLastIdle before UI-thread dispatch to
  avoid race with SessionIdleEvent on inactive chats
- Remove re-arm conditions (backgroundResultsHandledThisTurn,
  LastTurnWasAutoResume) from auto-resume scheduling to prevent
  infinite notification loops
- Add helper methods and unit tests for shell/agent ID parsing and
  completion detection

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
